trainFares.title: Intercity Rail Fare Comparison
trainFares.subtitle: How UK train prices compare to European peers
trainFares.source: Transport & Environment, Office of Rail and Road, national rail operators, Seat61
trainFares.methodology: Two comparison methods used: (1) Cost per kilometre (€/km) from Transport & Environment's 2024 European Rail Ranking, which analysed 8,000+ tickets across standardised journey types in 2nd class. Advance fares assume booking 4+ weeks ahead; day-of fares are walk-up prices. (2) Specific comparable intercity routes of approximately 250–350 km, showing both advance and flexible fares in 2nd class / standard. All prices converted to GBP at approximate March 2025 exchange rate (1 GBP = 1.17 EUR). Fare systems differ significantly: UK uses dynamic yield pricing with high walk-up premiums; Germany uses fixed regulated fares with moderate advance discounts; Netherlands uses flat-rate pricing; France and Spain use airline-style dynamic pricing.
limitations: Train fares are highly dynamic; prices change daily based on demand and booking date
€/km figures are aggregated averages from T&E study, not specific journeys
Route examples are indicative ranges, not guaranteed prices
Fare structures differ fundamentally: direct like-for-like comparison is approximate
UK fares include VAT at 0% (zero-rated); some European fares include local VAT
Railcard/discount card holders excluded from comparison

costPerKm.title: Cost per Kilometre by Country
costPerKm.subtitle: Average cost per km for standard 2nd class intercity journeys (€/km)

data.note: Largest gap between advance and walk-up fares in Europe

data.pricingModel: Fixed regulated fares; moderate advance discounts

sampleRoutes.title: Comparable Intercity Routes
sampleRoutes.subtitle: Approximate fares for ~250–350 km intercity journeys, 2nd class
sampleRoutes.note: Prices are indicative ranges based on spot checks and operator data. Actual fares vary by date, time, demand.

metroFares.title: Metro & Urban Transport Fare Comparison
metroFares.subtitle: How London TfL fares compare to peer city metro systems
metroFares.methodology: Primary comparison metric: single adult fare for a central-zone journey using the most common electronic payment method (contactless/card). All cities use off-peak or standard fare where peak/off-peak distinction exists (London off-peak Zone 1 Oyster/contactless). Tokyo and Singapore are distance-based: figures shown are the typical fare for a short central-area trip (3–5 stations), not the minimum fare. Monthly passes shown only where a genuine resident unlimited pass exists and is currently available. Airport fares excluded: airport pricing structures differ too fundamentally to compare. Exchange rates: 1 GBP = 1.17 EUR, 1 GBP = 1.27 USD, 1 GBP = 192 JPY, 1 GBP = 1.72 SGD (approximate, March 2025).
limitations: Zone systems are not directly comparable across cities: London uses concentric zones, Paris uses all-zone passes, Tokyo/Singapore are distance-based
Distance-based systems (Tokyo, Singapore) have no fixed single fare; figures are typical central-area trips
Monthly passes not available in all cities: NYC discontinued monthly passes (Jan 2026), Tokyo has no unlimited monthly, Singapore has no passes
Exchange rate fluctuations materially affect GBP equivalents
Peak vs off-peak: London shown as off-peak; other cities generally do not distinguish peak pricing for metro
Madrid monthly includes a temporary 40% government subsidy (extended through 2026); without subsidy the monthly would be €54.60

data.zoneNote: System-wide flat fare; OMNY contactless. 7-day rolling cap replaces monthly from Jan 2026
All Île-de-France zones. Navigo monthly pass is all-zone unlimited. No daily/weekly cap; pay-per-ride Liberté+ available
Zone A central. Monthly includes 40% extraordinary discount extended through 2026. Without discount: €54.60
Distance-based; ¥180 is minimum fare (1–3 stations). 24-hour pass ¥800 covers all metro lines. No weekly/monthly unlimited passes; commuter passes are route-specific
Zones AB (central Berlin). €49 Deutschlandticket covers all local/regional transport nationwide. Standard AB monthly subscription suspended Dec 2024
Distance-based, no caps or passes for residents. SGD 1.70 is typical CBD-area fare. Tourist passes available (SGD 17/day) but not comparable
